codeReview.summary: Reviewing the original failed diff (635fda2) and the rerun fix (adda4ac) shows real progress: the new commit threads prepared-source UUID refs through the filter boundary and uses them when marking input as sent. But it still does not fully resolve the blocker. Rust still derives those refs after the filter returns via `derive_filtered_input_source_indices()`, which falls back to heap-identity matches, unique fingerprints, sibling-order narrowing, and finally first-remaining assignment. Because the public filter API still returns only `ModelInputData`, a filter that drops/reorders siblings and replaces multiple items with fresh `InputItem`s can still be misattributed, so the tracker may mark the wrong original source as sent and later replay a delivered delta.

issues.description: `derive_filtered_input_source_indices()` still guesses filtered-item provenance instead of carrying explicit source identity end-to-end. The runtime path in `crates/agents-core/src/internal/turn_preparation.rs:36-46` calls this helper after `call_model_input_filter` returns, but the helper first matches by heap identity, then only unique fingerprints (`oai_conversation.rs:399-417`), then sibling-order heuristics (`oai_conversation.rs:419-447`), and finally assigns any remaining filtered items to `remaining_prepared.next()` (`oai_conversation.rs:450-453`). The filter API permits arbitrary fresh replacements because it returns only `ModelInputData` (`crates/agents-core/src/run_config.rs:23-25,169-170`), so a filter that replaces two reordered items with fresh values has no way to provide their exact prepared sources. In that case the fallback path can still mark the wrong original item as delivered and let the real source replay later. The new regression only proves a single anchored replacement case (`crates/agents-core/src/internal/oai_conversation.rs:659-699`), not the ambiguous multi-replacement case the feature description said must stop guessing. The JS reference still avoids this by carrying explicit `sourceItems`/`filterApplied` from the filter application result (`reference/openai-agents-js/packages/agents-core/src/runner/conversation.ts:36-49,133-169`).

summary: Fail. The rerun improves the old tracker-side remapping, but it still reconstructs filtered-item source refs with identity/fingerprint/order fallbacks instead of carrying exact provenance from the filter, so the original guessed-source blocker is not fully resolved.
